title: Dynamic Customer Pricing in Inventory
why: Price per unit is not static and depends on individual customers. Using a single static field caused incorrect data representation.
root_cause: The pricePerUnit field was designed as a static attribute of the product rather than a relation to CustomerPricing
solution: Added GET /api/inventory/product-pricing/:subSku. Replaced manual pricePerUnit edit with a dynamic popover showing how many customers have specific pricing for the subSku, along with their exact prices.
prevention: Always consider domain cross-overs (like CRM pricing vs Inventory) when designing data models
